task_completion.description: Open the dwanko_run.tnz scene in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is open, and the title bar, xsheet, or timeline clearly indicates that 'dwanko_run.tnz' is loaded.

task_completion.description: Apply a grayscale or desaturation effect to the animation scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz viewer shows the animation character in black and white (grayscale), OR the FX Schematic/Settings window is visible showing a grayscale, desaturate, or color adjustment node actively applied to the scene.

task_completion.description: Configure the render output directory to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' dialog is open in OpenToonz, and the 'Save In' or output folder field is set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/grayscale_test/'.

task_completion.description: Set the output format to PNG (or TGA) and ensure the frame range covers at least 12 frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Output Settings' dialog, the file format dropdown is set to 'png' (or 'tga'), and the 'From' / 'To' frame range is set to render a minimum of 12 frames (e.g., 1 to 12).

task_completion.description: Render the animation to produce the final grayscale image sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar or completed render popup is visible in OpenToonz, OR a file explorer/terminal window shows at least 12 generated image files in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/grayscale_test/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to apply the effect and render the frames, rather than using external command-line tools like FFmpeg or ImageMagick.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating OpenToonz menus, FX schematics, and Output Settings dialogs.

integrity.description: The grayscale effect was applied natively within the OpenToonz software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz viewer displays the grayscale effect, or FX nodes are visible in the interface, rather than the agent modifying the source files externally.

integrity.description: The agent actually rendered the files from OpenToonz rather than copying pre-existing files or mocking the output directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz render window or progress bar is visible, actively writing the frames to the target directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target scene is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz' (which is actually a 72-frame run cycle, despite the prompt calling it a walk cycle). The output directory must be exactly '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/grayscale_test/'. The agent must render at least 12 frames. Although the prompt specifies PNG, TGA format is also acceptable and should not be penalized. The output frames must be grayscale (maximum average color channel deviation of 5.0).
